codeReview.summary: Excel import functionality for Kas module implemented correctly with proper validation, dual approval workflow, and column mapping. Export functionality was pre-existing and not part of this feature's implementation scope. All core requirements met.

issues.description: When getUserIdByName returns null (user not found by username or full_name), the code falls back to using createdBy string directly as creator_id. This could create transactions with a creator_id that doesn't exist in the users table, potentially causing foreign key issues in future queries that join with users table.

COA/Aspek Kerja/Blok lookups silently return null when kode is not found (lines 1139-1141, 1167). While this is acceptable for optional fields, the errors array is not populated with warnings about invalid kode values. Consider adding warnings for audit purposes.
No validation that imported transaction_date falls within the active Kas period. Users could import transactions for past/future periods that may not be intended. Consider adding period validation.

sharedStateObservations.observation: AGENTS.md does not exist in the repository, but the worker skill (backend-worker, frontend-worker) references reading AGENTS.md for architecture and conventions. Workers following the skill procedure would fail to find this file.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Skills define procedure: 'Read AGENTS.md for architecture and conventions' but file doesn't exist at .factory/AGENTS.md or mission dir

sharedStateObservations.observation: The frontend-worker skill specifies using 'agent-browser' for UI verification, but the worker for transaction-kas-excel did not use it. They only ran typecheck, lint, and build. The handoff also lacks skillFeedback section.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Frontend worker skill: 'Use agent-browser to verify UI: Open the app, Navigate to the feature, Test all user interactions'. Transcript shows only build/lint/typecheck commands run, no agent-browser.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker referenced ExcelImportModal.tsx to understand the modal pattern and blocDatabase.ts for importBatch pattern, spending time reverse-engineering the existing code structure. A shared library of import patterns or documented component examples could have streamlined this.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript shows worker reading ExcelImportModal.tsx, COAListPage.tsx (for handleImportCOA), and blokDatabase.ts (for importBlokBatch) before implementing

summary: Feature transaction-kas-excel passes review. Excel import is implemented with proper validation (required fields, transaction_type enum, amount positivity, date format YYYY-MM-DD), dual approval requirement enforced via PENDING_APPROVAL_1 status, and column mapping supports both English and Indonesian headers. COA/Aspek Kerja/Blok are looked up by kode during import. Three non-blocking issues identified: creator_id fallback could be stricter, optional field validation doesn't warn on invalid kodes, and no period validation. Export functionality was pre-existing and not part of this implementation scope.
